design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: New topic: sharp partial identification for bunching designs when the running variable is observed only through deterministic rounding or heaping. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Saez/Chetty/Kleven bunching estimators and recent general bunching identification/inference papers, plus heaped-running-variable measurement-error work. Our theorem is not that because the estimand here is the aliasing frontier created by the coarsening operator: construct a finite signed-nullspace witness showing two latent densities with different bunching mass but identical rounded histograms, and a phase threshold in the heap-grid width versus excluded-window geometry for when any estimator can or cannot recover the mass. If the proposal reduces to generic deconvolution, polynomial counterfactual extrapolation, ordinary Manski bounds, or LP duality without an explicit aliasing certificate, pivot or stop early.

proposed_from.topic: New topic: sharp partial identification for bunching designs when the running variable is observed only through deterministic rounding or heaping. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Saez/Chetty/Kleven bunching estimators and recent general bunching identification/inference papers, plus heaped-running-variable measurement-error work. Our theorem is not that because the estimand here is the aliasing frontier created by the coarsening operator: construct a finite signed-nullspace witness showing two latent densities with different bunching mass but identical rounded histograms, and a phase threshold in the heap-grid width versus excluded-window geometry for when any estimator can or cannot recover the mass. If the proposal reduces to generic deconvolution, polynomial counterfactual extrapolation, ordinary Manski bounds, or LP duality without an explicit aliasing certificate, pivot or stop early.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue entries remain pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded, pid_rdd_flow_manipulation, pid_interval_censored_adoption, and pid_multivariate_interval_regression. None states a heap-specific bunching contrast-polytope frontier or a feasible-direction phase threshold for deterministic rounded histograms.","published_axis":"ManskiTamer2002IntervalData and BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011ConvexMoment supply general interval/random-set machinery, while BarrecaLindoWaddell2016HeapingRD, BoschDekkerStrohmaier2020Window, Dong2015RoundingRD, and Song2024GeneralBunching motivate heaping, window, rounding, and bunching identification. The revised gap is the heap-specific feasible aliasing frontier and its phase threshold on the observed histogram face."}
seed_list: Characterize the heap-aliasing frontier for rounded bunching histograms and certify nonidentification by a finite signed-nullspace witness.
Prove an equal-grid phase threshold for when heap width and excluded-window alignment make bunching mass informative or uninformative.
Show McCrary-style density diagnostics are not falsification-complete under deterministic heaping, and replace them with a signed-nullspace certificate test.
Give a restriction ladder showing endpoint pruning is sharp only under endpoint-cell dominance, not bounded curvature alone.
Construct a non-LP support-function representation of the rounded-score bunching identified set using heap-fiber nullspaces.
Show donut trimming can increase aliasing by deleting heap cells that separate bunching mass from counterfactual curvature.
Compare Song-style analytic point identification with deterministic coarsening and show analyticity after aggregation does not identify latent excess mass without a heap inverse condition.
Develop a finite-sample confidence set for the heap-aliasing frontier based on rounded multinomial counts and nullspace feasibility.
Characterize when notch missing-mass restrictions eliminate all heap-null directions and recover a point-identified excess-mass scalar.
Refute generic interval endpoint substitution for rounded bunching by exhibiting two latent refinements with identical endpoint intervals and different excess mass.
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","mode":"revise","version":3,"message":"Patched Conjecture 2 so the phase threshold is the rank of the feasible loading operator on the observed histogram face, with full-nullspace rank valid only under an interior feasible-face condition.","proposal_path":"D:/Stat/Causality/Lean Project/AutoID/CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/pid_bunching_heaping_phase/pid_bunching_heaping_phase_v1_proposal.tex","soundness_self_review":{"SC1_hypotheses_in_assumptions":"PASS — Conjectures 1 and 2 cite only assumptions now present in Section 7, including the new interior feasible-face assumption for the full-nullspace screen.","SC2_objects_defined":"PASS — D_G(q0), R^feas, R^full, tau*, A_G, Z_G, ell, and the feasible contrast polytope are defined before use.","SC3_corollaries_reduce":"PASS — the no-heaping corollary reduces because A_G is identity, Null(A_G) is zero, and the identified set is singleton.","SC4_intro_kernel_matches_§8":"PASS — Results-at-a-glance Conjecture 2 now matches Section 8's feasible loading operator rather than the full nullspace.","SC5_consumer_named":"PASS — Section 4 names heaped tax bunching designs and BoschDekkerStrohmaier2020Window as concrete consumers.","SC6_comparator_promise":"PASS — Song2024GeneralBunching, ManskiTamer2002IntervalData, BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011ConvexMoment, BarrecaLindoWaddell2016HeapingRD, and BoschDekkerStrohmaier2020Window are matched to Conjecture 1 or 2.","SC7_no_tautological_iff":"PASS — point-informativeness is characterized by zero feasible loading on A_G(q0)-A_G(q0), not by defining tau as the first value at which identification holds.","SC8_tuning_parameter_bounded":"PASS — no free epsilon-gap claim is used as a flagship threshold; seed_details.one_line: Characterize the heap-aliasing frontier for rounded bunching histograms and certify nonidentification by a finite signed-nullspace witness.

anchor_paper.author: Prior proposal pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded
anchor_paper.claim: Prior RDD manipulation proposal assumes observable cutoff-side weak limits rather than deterministic heap cell totals.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: P consists of the two cutoff-side weak limits P_- and P_+ of (Y,D,W) given the running variable

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/sharpness-failure is stronger because the witness targets named bunching/coarsening comparators and an open class of heap histograms.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ManskiTamer2002IntervalData because rounded scores are interval covariates.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap is the local excess-mass stencil: interval bounds do not give a heap-null certificate changing bunching mass while preserving rounded counts.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ManskiTamer2002IntervalData because the focal object is a signed aliasing frontier for a local bunching functional, not a generic regression interval set.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine convex support functions are used only after constructing the new heap-null certificate and aliasing frontier object.

adversarial_referee.instance: A generic LP frontier would miss the named aliasing certificate.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by naming and exhibiting the signed heap-null certificate in §9.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single four-cell witness could look hand-tuned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by Conjecture 1 promoting the witness to an open equal-grid class.

adversarial_referee.instance: Manski-Tamer might already cover interval coarsening.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by limiting the claim to bunching excess mass and deterministic heap nullspaces.

seed_details.one_line: Prove an equal-grid phase threshold for when heap width and excluded-window alignment make bunching mass informative or uninformative.

anchor_paper.author: Prior proposal pid_rdd_flow_manipulation
anchor_paper.claim: Prior many-window RDD flow attempt failed to compute the global endpoint envelope from primitives.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: computes only one fixed lambda,m slice, not the global lower/upper envelope from primitives

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift is stronger because it names a phase threshold in grid-window geometry, not a routine wider class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from finite-dimensional rank conditions because point identification is row-span membership.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nontrivial part is the geometric threshold tying row span to heap width, straddling cells, side curvature, and open feasibility.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not a generic rank condition because it conjectures an explicit heap-grid/excluded-window phase threshold with witness on the uninformative side.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: rank algebra produces a new threshold and witness class rather than a new-domain reuse.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity may be asserted only through examples.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal labels the iff as Conjecture and assigns necessity to signed-nullspace construction.

adversarial_referee.instance: Barreca et al. may already characterize heaping geometry.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Their claim concerns bias and diagnostics, not sharp identification phase thresholds.

adversarial_referee.instance: Threshold must be computable from observed primitives.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: §8 defines kappa and rho from A, B, r, M; §9 computes them on a small instance.

seed_details.one_line: Show McCrary-style density diagnostics are not falsification-complete under deterministic heaping, and replace them with a signed-nullspace certificate test.

anchor_paper.author: Prior proposal pid_interval_censored_adoption
anchor_paper.claim: Prior interval-censoring proposal supplies observational-equivalence template outside local bunching density functionals.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: two feasible refinements kappa_- and kappa_+ induce the same law P(Y,C)

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends witness method to a new setting, stronger because McCrary density tests provably miss heap-null latent perturbations.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just another observational equivalence witness from the failed interval-censoring proposal.
seed_details.rebuttal: The target functional is local excess density around a heap-straddled bunching window, not event-time adoption refinement.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not pid_interval_censored_adoption_v1 because the same observed histogram hides a local density jump/excess-mass contrast.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the certificate becomes a falsification procedure for deterministic heaping, not only an existence witness.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Certificate feasibility is the replacement diagnostic.

adversarial_referee.instance: A nullspace witness could be invisible only at one law.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need open-class promotion; secondary seed, not chosen.
The seed positions McCrary as diagnostic benchmark, not as heaping estimator.

seed_details.one_line: Give a restriction ladder showing endpoint pruning is sharp only under endpoint-cell dominance, not bounded curvature alone.

anchor_paper.author: Prior proposal pid_multivariate_interval_regression
anchor_paper.claim: Prior interval-regression failure warns that frontier pruning needs endpoint-cell dominance.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: frontier pruning dropped non-frontier lower/upper cells without an endpoint-cell stochastic-dominance

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption weakening is stronger because it names the failure mode of endpoint pruning and the dominance condition that fixes it.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from multivariate interval regression endpoint-pruning failure.
seed_details.rebuttal: The bunching stencil makes nonendpoint mass locally causal for excess density, so the dominance condition differs from regression endpoint dominance.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not pid_multivariate_interval_regression_v1 because the endpoint error is induced by local heap fibers around a bunching window.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the restriction ladder names which signed-nullspace witnesses survive each shape restriction.

adversarial_referee.instance: Endpoint dominance may only be sufficient.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 2 states necessity for endpoint pruning under three-subcell heap fibers.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could identify multiple bunching functionals jointly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands partly; field-to-flagship upgrade should state the full family.

adversarial_referee.instance: Random-set convex moment methods may subsume it.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: They do not state the endpoint-pruning validity frontier for bunching stencils.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a non-LP support-function representation of the rounded-score bunching identified set using heap-fiber nullspaces.

anchor_paper.author: Beresteanu, Molchanov, Molinari
anchor_paper.claim: Characterizes sharp identification regions for convex moment predictions and interval data.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP avoided by using a support-function/nullspace representation tailored to deterministic heap fibers.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from support functions for convex sets.
seed_details.rebuttal: The novelty is not the support function alone but the heap-fiber nullspace basis for local excess-mass stencils.

seed_details.one_line: Show donut trimming can increase aliasing by deleting heap cells that separate bunching mass from counterfactual curvature.

anchor_paper.author: Noack and Rothe
anchor_paper.claim: Donut RD can have larger bias and variance than conventional RD.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary shift is stronger if trimming provably removes identifying constraints; weaker if only another bias example.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a donut-RD bias observation.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed targets identification loss from deleting coarsening constraints, not mean-squared-error bias.

seed_details.one_line: Compare Song-style analytic point identification with deterministic coarsening and show analyticity after aggregation does not identify latent excess mass without a heap inverse condition.

anchor_paper.author: Myunghyun Song
anchor_paper.claim: General bunching point identification relies on analyticity of the counterfactual density.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Refutation/sharpening is stronger if analyticity of aggregated histograms is shown insufficient by heap-null perturbations.
seed_details.referee_objection: Analyticity of the latent density may already rule out the perturbation.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed distinguishes latent analyticity from smoothness of rounded cell totals; the inverse condition is the missing object.

seed_details.one_line: Develop a finite-sample confidence set for the heap-aliasing frontier based on rounded multinomial counts and nullspace feasibility.

anchor_paper.author: Kolesar and Rothe
anchor_paper.claim: Discrete running variable confidence intervals can have poor coverage with moderate support.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimator/inference extension is weaker unless paired with the aliasing frontier; retained as field-tier follow-up.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is standard multinomial inference for a finite-dimensional identified set.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; useful but not the flagship kernel without the phase theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize when notch missing-mass restrictions eliminate all heap-null directions and recover a point-identified excess-mass scalar.

anchor_paper.author: Kleven and Waseem
anchor_paper.claim: Uses excess bunching and missing mass around notches to identify frictions and structural elasticities.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition is stronger if notch missing-mass restrictions remove exactly the heap-nullspace directions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine specialization of notch bunching restrictions.
seed_details.rebuttal: The strict delta is the exact nullspace-elimination condition under deterministic rounding.

seed_details.one_line: Refute generic interval endpoint substitution for rounded bunching by exhibiting two latent refinements with identical endpoint intervals and different excess mass.

anchor_paper.author: Manski and Tamer
anchor_paper.claim: Provides interval-data partial-identification bounds without restrictions on unobserved values inside intervals.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample is weaker if a single law only; stronger when promoted to a straddling-cell class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is exactly what interval-data bounds already say.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed targets invalid endpoint substitution for a local bunching stencil, not the existence of interval uncertainty.

proposed_from.literature_map: Saez (2010, AEJ Policy) and Chetty-Friedman-Olsen-Pistaferri (2011, QJE) anchor kink bunching by translating excess mass into elasticity through a smooth counterfactual density. Kleven-Waseem (2013, QJE) add notches, where missing mass and dominated regions provide extra identifying structure. Bertanha-McCallum-Seegert (2021, FEDS) warns that flexible heterogeneity weakens kink identification and makes assumptions central. Bertanha-McCallum-Payne-Seegert (2022, Stata Journal) operationalize bunching estimators and stress that assumptions on the unobserved distribution are not data-verifiable. Song (2024, arXiv) gives general point and partial identification for bunching, with analyticity central for point identification. Bosch-Jongen-Lejour (2020, International Tax and Public Finance) show bunching-window choices matter. Barreca-Lindo-Waddell (2016, Economic Inquiry) is the closest heaping warning: usual RD diagnostics may miss non-random heaping, and bias depends on bandwidth and heap location. Dong (2015, JAE), Davezies-Le Barbanchon (2017, JoE), and Dong-Kolesar (2023, JAE) cover rounded or mismeasured RD running variables, but not bunching-specific heap aliasing. McCrary (2008, JoE), Kolesar-Rothe (2018, AER), and Noack-Rothe (2023, arXiv) cover manipulation tests, discrete-support inference, and donut trimming; none gives an aliasing frontier for rounded bunching histograms. Manski-Tamer (2002, Econometrica) and Beresteanu-Molchanov-Molinari (2011, Econometrica) provide interval/coarsening partial-identification machinery, but not finite signed-nullspace witnesses for bunching mass. The live gap is the sharp relationship between deterministic heap fibers, excluded-window geometry, and local excess-mass functionals.

banked_reason: Stopped after two D0.5 REVISE reviews: theorem contract still omitted endpoint formula, convex/polyhedral feasibility was missing for the sharp interval claim, and novelty remained field-tier generic LP/nullspace rather than flagship heap-grid phase structure.
